title: ITU AI for Good: Micro Drones and AI Powering Precision Agriculture Toward Zero Hunger
detail: The ITU AI for Good initiative hosted a session on April 19 exploring how micro drone technology combined with AI analytics is being deployed for precision agriculture across the developing world, directly contributing to SDG 2 (Zero Hunger) goals. AI-equipped drones are being used for crop health monitoring in smallholder farming communities — identifying disease outbreaks, nutrient deficiencies, and water stress at field-level resolution that satellite imagery cannot achieve. In sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia, micro drone deployments are helping community agricultural extension workers cover more farms more thoroughly than is possible on foot, while AI analysis translates imagery into actionable guidance farmers can implement. The session highlighted case studies from Kenya, Rwanda, and Bangladesh where drone-AI combinations have reduced crop losses and improved yields for smallholders operating on marginal land under increasing climate stress.

media.caption: ITU AI for Good: micro drones and AI for precision agriculture toward zero hunger

title: ITU Robotics for Good Youth Challenge Egypt — AI Innovation Hub for North African Youth
detail: The ITU Robotics for Good Youth Challenge came to Egypt on April 19, convening young innovators in one of Africa's largest and most influential technology markets. Egypt has been positioning itself as a regional AI hub, with national AI strategy investments and a large youth population (60% under 30) representing an enormous potential AI talent base. Participants engaged with AI-powered robotics challenges focused on agriculture, healthcare access, and climate adaptation — problems Egypt faces acutely with its Nile Delta food security pressures, rapidly growing urban population, and water scarcity. The challenge connects Egyptian youth to ITU's global AI for Good network and provides pathways to the main AI for Good Global Summit in Geneva in July 2026, where youth innovators from across the Global South present solutions to international audiences.

media.caption: ITU Robotics for Good Youth Challenge Egypt — AI innovation for North African youth
